data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260511T021223-a368dc99/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260511T021223-a368dc99/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260511T021223-a368dc99/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260511T021223-a368dc99/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260511T021223-a368dc99/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-73c07039">
    <title>feat(metrics): reviewer accuracy tracking — record false-positive/negative for reviewer-quality drift detection</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  The reviewer is a load-bearing gate: it BLOCKs fake-work and
  APPROVEs real-work. But the reviewer is itself an AI model
  whose performance can drift (prompt regressions, model
  version changes, distribution shifts in bead content).
  Today there is no signal on reviewer accuracy — we cannot
  tell whether the reviewer is BLOCKing real work as
  false-positives or APPROVing fake work as false-negatives.

  Three observed fake-work cases (ddx-1f774edf, ddx-94a368b6,
  ddx-d22293ba/ddx-cccb4763) on 2026-05-10 were reviewer
  false-negatives. We caught them by manual coherency review;
  there's no system signal that would have alerted us.

  Closing this loop requires a feedback signal: when the
  reviewer's verdict diverges from the eventual truth, record
  it.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go produces verdicts
    APPROVE | BLOCK. No accuracy telemetry.
  - The metrics extraction bead (ddx-054b2449, queued) will
    record review_verdict per attempt, but not whether that
    verdict was correct.
  - No mechanism captures the operator's eventual override
    (e.g., a BLOCKed bead the operator manually closes; an
    APPROVEd bead the operator later reopens for fake-work).

PROPOSED FIX
  Add an "review accuracy event" emitted when the reviewer's
  verdict is contradicted by a later authoritative signal.

  Sources of authoritative signal:

  1. **Operator override**: operator manually transitions a
     reviewer-BLOCKed bead to status=closed (without further
     work). Or operator reopens a reviewer-APPROVEd bead with
     a "fake-work" or "rework" reason.
     - Hook: cli/internal/bead/store.go Close/Reopen paths.
     - Emit: "review-accuracy-override" event on the bead, with
       fields {verdict_was, operator_action, reason}.

  2. **Downstream test/build failure on landed work**: if a
     reviewer-APPROVED bead's land introduces a build break or
     test failure within N days, that's a false-negative.
     - Hook: post-merge CI signal (when available) OR a
       periodic sweep that diffs landed beads against
       eventual main HEAD.
     - Out of scope for v1 — rely on operator-override signal.

  3. **AC-check disagreement**: when ac-check.json says fail
     but the reviewer issued APPROVE, that is a likely
     false-negative. The sister bead ddx-a9ff01a1 (reviewer
     ratifies ac-check) requires the reviewer to OVERRIDE
     ac-check with explicit reason; this bead records those
     overrides for later auditing.
     - Hook: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go,
       at verdict emission time.
     - Emit: "review-ac-override" event when reviewer's
       per-AC grade contradicts ac-check.json's mechanical
       result.

  Per-attempt fields added to the metrics row (extends
  ddx-054b2449's schema):
    - reviewer_acmismatch_count: int — number of ACs where
      reviewer and ac-check disagree
    - reviewer_override_reasons: list[string] — reasons cited
      in judgment_override
    - reviewer_accuracy_signal: enum {confirmed, override,
      contested, unknown} — set by the operator-override hook
      after the fact

  `ddx work analyze --by reviewer-model` (extends ddx-09ceb430):
  query the metrics file to surface reviewer false-positive /
  false-negative rates by model. Cross-reference signals tell
  us when to iterate on the reviewer prompt or escalate
  reviewer routing.

  Tests:
    - TestReviewAccuracy_OperatorOverride_EmitsEvent
    - TestReviewAccuracy_ACCheckDisagreement_RecordedInMetrics
    - TestAnalyze_ByReviewerModel_SurfacesFPRate

NON-SCOPE
  - Downstream test/build failure as a signal (v1 uses
    operator-override + ac-check disagreement; CI integration
    is a future bead).
  - Auto-tuning reviewer routing based on accuracy (separate
    concern).
  - UI/dashboard for accuracy time series (separate concern).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Depends on ddx-054b2449 (metrics extraction) for the row
    schema.
  - Depends on ddx-09ceb430 (analyze CLI) for the query
    interface.
  - Depends on ddx-a9ff01a1 (reviewer ratifies ac-check) for
    the AC-check disagreement signal.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/bead/store.go Close and Reopen paths emit a "review-accuracy-override" event when the operator's action contradicts the reviewer's prior verdict, with fields {verdict_was, operator_action, reason}.
2. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go emits a "review-ac-override" event when the reviewer's per-AC grade disagrees with the ac-check.json mechanical result.
3. The metrics row schema (per ddx-054b2449) gains reviewer_acmismatch_count, reviewer_override_reasons, reviewer_accuracy_signal fields.
4. `ddx work analyze --by reviewer-model` (per ddx-09ceb430) surfaces per-model false-positive and false-negative rates.
5. TestReviewAccuracy_OperatorOverride_EmitsEvent, TestReviewAccuracy_ACCheckDisagreement_RecordedInMetrics, TestAnalyze_ByReviewerModel_SurfacesFPRate all pass.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, area:review, area:metrics, kind:feature, observability</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="5228d59c6f3ed17032b37f80de05165c88162d1c" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260511T021223-a368dc99"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>
